entry.title: Re: Batch management at 'Plant level and 'Material level'...
entry.content: If both are ticked, batch management is enforced globally. The plant‑level flag becomes a mirror of the material‑level flag, not an independent setting. The impact is that batch numbers are unique across all plants, and every plant must manage batches for that material.

entry.title: Jetpack Compose FioriSimpleTextField
entry.content:
Hello Community,

When using the SAP Fiori Jetpack Compose UI library there is the FioriSimpleTextField Component:

https://help.sap.com/doc/f53c64b93e5140918d676b927a3cd65b/Cloud/en-US/docs-en/guides/features/fiori-ui/android/compose/simple-text-field.html

The issue is that it forces you to have a label.
This is what it looks like with a label:

This is what it looks like when passing an empty string:

As you can see there is a bunch of whitespace where the label would be. That means the component is really forcing you to use a label.

Is there a workaround here to remove a label?

Would it be possible to make the label nullable so that we can only display the input field?

Thank you for your help.

entry.title: Quality Gates: Leveraging the continuous quality check (CQC) Service Report for Project Readiness
entry.content:
Quality Gates are a core component of the SAP Activate methodology, designed to ensure projects move forward in a structured, controlled, and high-quality way. Each Quality Gate serves as a formal checkpoint at the end of a phase, confirming that the project is ready to transition to the next phase with the right foundations in place.

What brings consistency, scalability, and real insight into this process, particularly for SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Activate for SAP SuccessFactors, is the continuous quality check (CQC) for implementation gates service report. Rather than relying on manual validation alone, the CQC service report enables a standardized, data-driven assessment of project readiness, ensuring alignment with SAP best practices and SAP Activate methodology.  

Within SAP Cloud ALM, the Quality Gate feature is used to:

  • Conduct the associated Quality Gate
  • Submit checklist responses for evaluation
  • Request the CQC for implementation gates service report (only for Prepare and Explore phases)

Once submitted, the CQC service report is automatically generated based on the checklist inputs. It delivers tailored recommendations specific to your project.

The results are fully integrated within SAP Cloud ALM:

  • CQC service results
  • Downloadable PDF report
  • Standalone action items

All of these are accessible within the Service pillar, providing a centralized location for managing quality insights and follow-up actions.

The CQC service report is a structured output generated as part of the Quality Gate service. It consolidates:

  • Responses to Quality Gate questions
  • Identified risks, gaps, and recommendations
  • A summarized readiness assessment

It serves as a single source of truth for the Quality Gate evaluation, particularly for the Prepare and Explore phases, and supports the decision on whether the project is ready to proceed to the next phase.

The CQC service report enhances the process by:

Standardizing the Assessment

Ensures all projects are evaluated using consistent SAP-defined criteria.

Ensuring Alignment to Best Practices

Validates that the project adheres to SAP Activate methodology and recommended implementation standards.

Identifying Risks Early

Highlights gaps and risks that could impact downtime phases if not addressed.

The report is designed to deliver value to both executive stakeholders and the project team, typically including:

  • Key Findings: A concise summary of the most important insights for stakeholders.
    • Positive Findings: Areas where SAP best practices are being successfully applied.
    • Significant Findings: Issues requiring remediation or course correction.
    • Critical Findings: Critical issues requiring remediation or course correction.
  • Action Plan: Prioritized actions with a clear priority and deadlines.
  • Assessment Results: Detailed responses to Quality Gate questions.

The Conduct Quality Gate task is no longer just a milestone, it is an opportunity to validate, align, and strengthen your project using real insights. By leveraging the CQC service report within SAP Cloud ALM, project teams can ensure they are not only completing required activities but doing so in alignment with SAP best practices. This enables a confident transition into the next phase, aligned, prepared, and set up for success.

entry.title: SAP SF LMS external (functional) users managing - future termination date
entry.content:
Hi community.

We have external/functionsl users in SAP SF LMS. Such users are temporary, so in most cases we know the term of their work, for example user A work from 1 September 2025 till 31 March 2026.

We import such users to the LMS manually via User Connector. It woul be nice to import in advance hire data 1 September 2025 and termination date  31 March 2026 (so on the 31 March 2026 SAP SF automatically make user as inactive). But it is impossible to import future termination date in SAP SF LMS, so we need to have some excel and almost every week prepare files with terminated employees for import to LMS via connector. Such approach is very manual and takes much time.

 

Maybe you can share some other approch, that is used in your companies?

Thanks.

entry.title: How to: Use OpenText Core Archive for Document Storage/Archiving from SAP BTP / Build?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We are designing a solution on SAP BTP using SAP Build / CAP and SAP Build Process Automation and would like to use OpenText Core Archive for SAP Solutions as our document storage/archiving platform – without involving an SAP ECC/S/4HANA backend for the document upload.

Scenario:

  • We have a SAP Fiori / CAP front-end application with a file upload (PDF) functionality deployed in Cloud Foundry / BTP.
  • Uploaded PDFs should:
    1. Be stored/archived centrally in OpenText Core Archive for SAP Solutions.
    2. Be retrievable later in SAP Build Process Automation to show the document (PDF) in an approval form for review/approval.
  • We want to call OpenText directly from BTP, not via ECC/S/4HANA, and then access those documents via APIs from workflows in SAP Build Process Automation.

Questions:

  • OpenText Core Archive APIs from BTP

    • Does OpenText Core Archive for SAP Solutions expose REST APIs that can be called directly from BTP (CAP/Node/Java) for:
      • Creating (uploading) documents (PDF) with metadata?
      • Retrieving documents (either as binary or via a time-limited URL)?
  • Or is there a more “standard” pattern for using Core Archive as a central document store from BTP without ECC/S/4?
  • Are there any official guidelines or recommendations from SAP/OpenText for this type of BTP ↔ Core Archive integration?
  • Even if examples are for Extended ECM or other OpenText products, any patterns applicable to Core Archive would be helpful.

Any inputs, experiences, example architectures, or links to documentation/blogs are highly appreciated.

Thanks! in Advance.

BR,
Rakesh

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA: Data model change for BOM and Routing validity
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA introduced a new field into the BOM tables, in order to support a better performance during the BOM Explosion. A field called VALID_TO has been introduced in the BOM Header table STPO and in the BOM Item level table STKO, and they were introduced to minimize the complex calculation required to determine the BOM header and component validity, especially when the BOM is changed with a change number.

 A correct value for those fields is required for the correct BOM explosion and the calculation of the validity date in the following scenarios:

  • BOM transactions that were optimized for a better performance on HANA (CS11H / CS12H / CS13H)
  • Fiori app Maintain bill of material (F1813)
  • API API_BILL_OF_MATERIAL_SRV
  • MRP Live
  • Report CS_BOM_PRODVER_MIGRATION02

If those fields are empty, for example, MRP Live will not be able to correctly calculate the validity of BOMs and BOM components that were changed with a change number, as explained in SAP KBA 2611243, and the planning results may be incorrect.  

The routing tables were also changed, and fields VALID_TO and LOEKZ_INHERITED were added to the database tables, PLKO, PLFL, PLPO, PLAS, PLMZ, PLFH, PLMK, PLMW and MAPL.

This blog explains what can be done to avoid this issue when migrating to SAP S/4HANA.

Determine BOM Explosion Mode in Customizing

A new customizing setting has been introduced in SAP S/4HANA, in order to determine the BOM Explosion Mode. SAP S/4HANA should be able to correctly determine the values for those fields, as long as the correct value is set for Determine Explosion Mode for BOM in HANA system. We can access this customizing activity in the SPRO path Production - Basic data - Bill of material - Control data for bill of material - Determine Explosion Mode, as shown in the figure below:

Figure 1: Determine Explosion Mode customizing.

In this customizing activity, we need to and an entry for the BOM category 'Enable BOM Validity Calculation' and choose the option 'Enable BOM Validity Calculation', as shown in the following Figure:

Figure 2: Enable BOM validity calculation


This customizing setting will ensure that the BOM validity is correctly updated in STKO and STPO, whenever the BOM is changed with a change number, and SAP KBA 2516380 provides more information about this customizing step.

Update the validity in existing BOMs

The customizing change mentioned above will take care of newly created BOMs, but those fields will not be updated for BOMs that were previously created or changed with change numbers. In order to update those fields, report CS_BOM_VALID_TO_CALC should be executed in the system.

This report should be executed twice. The first time, with the flag STKO (Validity for BOM Header) set, as shown in the figure below:

Figure 3: Validity for BOM Header

And the second time with the flag STPO (Validity for BOM Item) marked, as shown below:

Figure 4: Validity for BOM Item

Note: We can control the number of parallel processes to be used in section Control Parameters of the report. System will propose a number of processes, but we can reduce, to limit system resource consumption, or increase to improve the performance. The system admin should be able to suggest an optimal number of processes to be used. 

The can also be executed for specific materials, and if the flag Validity for all BOMs is marked is set, the report will show input fields for the material and the plant.

Update the validity of existing Routings

In order to ensure that the routing operations will be correctly read by relevant applications, fields VALID_TO and LOEKZ_INHERITED should be populated into the database tables, PLKO, PLFL, PLPO, PLAS, PLMZ, PLFH, PLMK, PLMW and MAPL. Report CP_ADAPT_TASKLIST_TO_CDSVIEW should be executed in the SAP S/4HANA to populate those fields. 

Figure 5: Report CP_ADAPT_TASKLIST_TO_CDSVIEW

The Information button in the report provides the following information, which explains how each field should be used:

Conclusion

Running those reports in the system is a one-time activity, and once the customizing activity has been completed, all the required fields should be populated and the newly created BOMs and Routings should have the correct values in the tables, if the customizing setting has been correctly updated. 

 

Additional Information:

3058214- BOM valid to date inconsistency in t-code CS03 and table STKO/STPO
3691769 - Error when changing BOM with new ECN in Fiori APP F1813. 
3688932 - Manage Multilevel Bill of Material Fiori app shows several occurrences of single material
3664897 - API_BILL_OF_MATERIAL_SRV_0002 is not consistently returning "ValidityEndDate"
3619956 - Material Where-Used List in BOMs (F2214) App shows different result as classic t-code CS15/CS15M
2543040 - CS11H: Error CS_BOM_HANA001 Parameter Combination is not valid
2611243 - T-code MD01N doesn't consider BOM changes made with change numbers
3305612 - Report CS_BOM_PRODVER_MIGRATION02 cannot select specific BOM/routing
3266015 -  Routing valid to date inconsistency in t-code CA03 and table PLKO/PLPO

 

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA Regional Implementation Group

entry.title: Incomplete Results in App Purchase Order Items by Account Assignment
entry.content:
In this blog post, I would like to share an issue we encountered in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud where several users mentioned missing Purchase Orders and incomplete results in the App Purchase Order Items by Account Assignment. This may help others facing similar inconsistencies in text determination.

 

Issue:

When using the App Purchase Order Items by Account Assignment, certain Purchase Orders / Purchase Order Items can not be found.

 

Root Cause:

According to Purchase Order Items by Account Assignment | SAP Help Portal, only purchase order items that are still open are displayed. Those that are completely delivered / invoiced are not displayed.

 

Solution:

If you cannot find a specific Purchase Order / Purchase Order Item, open the relevant document in the App Manage Purchase Orders and check for the indicators Is Fully Delivered and Is Invoiced. These statuses determine whether an item is considered open and therefore displayed in the reporting app.

 

Summary:

Missing items in the Purchase Order Items by Account Assignment app are often caused by item status rather than configuration inconsistencies. Checking delivery and invoice status typically resolves the issue.

entry.title: What is a Data Steward?
entry.content:
A data steward is the business-facing guardian of an organization’s data: the person who turns fragmented, messy information into reliable, understandable, and defensible assets. They operate at the intersection of business and technology, ensuring that data is fit for purpose for reporting, analytics, and AI while making it easy for people across the organization to find, interpret, and trust the numbers they use to make decisions.

 

What they do

Rather than a long list of tasks, think of a data steward’s work as three connected threads. First, they set and enforce standards: defining business terms, documenting lineage, and embedding governance so answers are reproducible and explainable. Second, they improve quality and access by profiling data, fixing or escalating issues, and curating business-ready data products that reduce manual preparation. Third, they enable people—collaborating with IT, domain experts, analysts, and governance teams to ensure the right controls, ownership, and context are in place so decisions can be defended under scrutiny.

Why it matters

Without stewardship, organizations face competing answers, wasted time reconciling datasets, and increased regulatory or reputational risk. With effective stewardship, teams spend less time validating numbers and more time acting on insights. Trusted data shortens time-to-insight, reduces duplicate work, and makes advanced uses of data—like production AI—both safer and more reliable.

Common challenges

Data stewards contend with legacy debt, siloed systems, and tool sprawl that force manual stitching and reconciliation. Governance is often seen as a blocker rather than a safeguard, and many tools create visual outputs without preserving lineage or enforceable ownership. The most effective stewards focus on building governance into workflows, so documentation, monitoring, and lineage are captured automatically instead of retrofitted.

Tools and approaches that help

Practical stewardship relies on active metadata and an enterprise data catalog to make assets discoverable and transparent, a unified semantic layer so everyone shares meanings and metrics, and master data management to keep core reference data consistent. Governance capabilities that are embedded in the data lifecycle, plus automated profiling, monitoring, and AI-assisted mapping of lineage, reduce manual effort and make controls scalable. The goal is to simplify architecture rather than add yet another layer of tools.

Skills and traits

Successful data stewards combine domain knowledge, attention to detail, and technical fluency with strong communication and stakeholder influence. They translate between business questions and technical requirements, prioritize fixes based on impact, and run data literacy programs so users can interpret and defend results.

Getting started (practical first moves)

  • Inventory key sources and stakeholders and agree on a small set of core business definitions.
  • Profile data to find the highest-impact quality issues and capture lineage and ownership.
  • Put cataloging, basic monitoring, and governance rules in place, then iterate based on usage and outcomes.

In short, a data steward makes data discoverable, explainable, and defensible—embedding governance so teams can move quickly without sacrificing trust. With the right mix of tools and practices, stewardship transforms data from a liability into an asset that supports faster, safer decision-making and more reliable AI.

entry.title: Demystifying Model Context Protocol (MCP), Part 1: The Foundation
entry.content:
Introduction

Model Context Protocol (MCP) is quickly becoming the standard way for AI agents to talk to APIs and data sources. Today, many projects wire LLMs to backends with custom, one‑off glue code that is hard to reuse or share.

MCP changes this by defining a common language for tools and resources that any compatible agent can understand with the help of LLMs.

In this three‑part series:

  1. In Part-1, we will look at what MCP is, why it matters and when it is worth adopting in your landscape.
  2. In Part 2, we will build and deploy a commerce MCP server on SAP BTP and
  3. In Part 3, we will connect that MCP server to Joule Agent Builder to power real agentic scenarios.

What is MCP?

By now, everyone knows the full form of MCP - Model Context Protocol. Let us understand some more details and building blocks which makes MCP talk of the town nowadays.

Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard through which AI applications connect to external enterprise systems,  APIs, databases and object stores. MCP wraps the complexity of heterogeneous systems and abstracts integrations away from the Agentic solutions so that agentic solutions can focus of AI piece and spend less time on integrations.

Metaphor:  Think of MCP as a universal electric adapter. Every country has its own outlet shape just as every system exposes its own API style. With MCP,  Agentic AI applications only needs one adapter. MCP defines a consistent way to describe tools and resources to plug into many different systems without integrating each one from scratch.

Building blocks of MCP

  1. Host Application + MCP Client: This is basically your agentic AI solution which embeds MCP client that talks to the MCP server on behalf of the LLM.  MCP client discovers available tools exposed by the MCP server and routes the LLM's request for data to the MCP server.
  2. MCP Server: MCP server is the application or a program which exposes the capabilities of your application or enterprise system to the host application through MCP standards.
  3. Tools: Tools are actions that the MCP server can perform. For example: "Get Product List", "Create Cart", "Add Product to Cart" etc. Tools gives host applications a structured set of capabilities.
  4. Transport Layer: MCP uses standard communication like STDIO for local and HTTP with Server‑Sent Events for remote servers.

The diagram below gives a overview of how MCP acts as a facade for the agentic AI solutions.

Figure-1

Now as we understand the "What", let us jump into "Why".

Why MCP matter?

In this section, I have listed down 3 main advantages MCP brings on the table

  1. Reduce custom integrations and increase reusability: Without MCP every AI application would build it's own integration and adapters to talk to heterogeneous APIs. MCP standardizes how tools and APIs are exposed.
  2. Provide access to data: LLMs require data to provide valuable outputs. Agents can provide this data to LLMs using MCP tools. This helps to increase the accuracy of the agentic solution and reduce hallucinations.
  3. Better managed security: MCP servers act like a facade to your enterprise data and resources. You can think of centralizing the authentication, authorization and audit logging for the underlying systems.
  4. Scale AI solutions: As MCP servers mature, you can add more tools which indirectly adds capabilities to your host application.

When it's worth to adopt MCP?

By now, you have got a good idea of MCP, but should we start building MCP server for every agentic solution? This section outlines some pointers - when MCP makes sense.

  1. When you need one integration used by many agents in your enterprise.
  2. When you want to access heterogeneous systems in a consistent way.
  3. When your agentic solution depends on real enterprise data
  4. When your agentic AI landscape is likely to grow and you do not want to reinvent integrations.

Brainstorming the need of MCP server is very important otherwise it can be one more additional code base for the team to maintain and scale.

Wrap-up

At this point, we have looked at 

  • What is Model Context Protocol.
  • The problem it solves in ever growing agentic world.
  • Building blocks of MCP
  • When it is worth adopting MCP in your enterprise landscape.

In Part-2 of the series, we will move from theory to code. I will build a MCP server, run it locally and then deploy it on SAP BTP and we can see how it can power real agentic use cases.

Disclaimer: This is not an official reference application or documentation. The thoughts outlined in this blog are based on my real world experience and learnings.

Feel free to “like“, “Share“, “Add a Comment” and to get more updates about my next blogs follow me!

References

  1. https://www.anthropic.com/news/model-context-protocol
  2. https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/learn/architecture

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Pack: How to check it in your system going forward?
entry.content:
@Shwethamani_M @jurgen_pirotte @stefan_berndt  I think this blog needs to be reviewed and checked for accuracy but also readability.

The fact that the CP check report returns simplification items as "relevant", the orange triangle, doesn't necessarily mean a potential contract violation. The CP checks are very basic and rudimentary. For example, the check for SI1: HR_OP only checks the number of entries in table PA0000 (>0) and whether the business function H4S4_1 is inactive. Any given ECC system where HCM was previously used would be this way, unless a clean up was done. H4S4_1 is inactive by default because activating it requires a license. The use of HR mini-master is allowed as per SAP note 3369920 and that includes entries in PA0000 so I would question the accuracy of the check.

My point is, customers have to look at each SI one at a time and assess whether the reported relevancy for simplification items are false-positives or actual findings. This will take time and resources. While the report is helpful, it is just the beginning.

1. Screenshot of the payment document where the business area is required.

3. Payment document generated by the automatic payment program

After making the changes mentioned above, I ran the automatic payment program. Although the Business area field was made required, the program still completed the payment run and did not populate the business area on the payment document, as shown in the screenshot in this question.

Can anyone please advise how I can make the Business area field required and copy the business area from the original invoice to the payment document? highly appreciate.

Faraz

entry.title: Re: Minimum Roles and Authorization for Technical user fo...
entry.content:
Set-up the role and user
 Create custom role ZCUS_DI_ABAP_REMOTE based on standard role SAP_DI_ABAP_REMOTE
 Create custom role ZCUS_DI_ABAP_USER based on standard role SAP_DI_ABAP_USER

For connection user which should be system user provide ZCUS_DI_ABAP_REMOTE, to the end user provide ZCUS_DI_ABAP_USER role.

also the user which is responsible for running the jobs in S4 Hana ON Premis also provide 

S_DMIS
MBT_PR_ARE = SLOP
MBT_PR_LEV = PACKAGE
ACTVT = 16

you will be good to go.

regards,

Zeeshan

 

Regards,

entry.title: Can we develop a custom program to execute SAPAPO/OM17 Live cache consistency check in bckground
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are currently performing the APO LiveCache consistency check manually using transaction /SAPAPO/OM17 on a daily basis after the required SYNC jobs are completed.

Our current manual process is:

  1. Verify SYNC jobs completion in SM37
  2. Run transaction /SAPAPO/OM17
  3. Execute Consistency Check LiveChache
  4. Evaluate the last background job
  5. Check inconsistencies
  6. Correct inconsistencies

Since this is a repetitive daily activity, we are exploring whether this can be automated.

We understand that /SAPAPO/OM17 is an interactive transaction, so directly background execution may not be straightforward. We also tried exploring recording options, but that does not seem reliable.

Our questions are:

  • Is it technically possible to develop a custom background for this requirement?
  • Are there any standard reports/ function modules/classes used internally by /SAPAPO/OM17 that can be called from a custom program?
  • Has anyone implemented a similar automation for OM17 or Live Cache consistency checks?
  • What would be the recommended approach: Custom wrapper program, background job chain, or any standard SAP-supported option?

Our main goal is to reduce manual effort and make the consistency check process more stable and repeatable.

Any suggestion or guidance would be very helpful.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: FuncionalLocationName field is always empty on API_FU...
entry.content:
Dear @saraserrano,

In Public Cloud an empty FunctionalLocationName in API_FUNCTIONALLOCATION is a known standard limitation and is usually related to language-dependent description handling, not to a communication error. The field is returned based on the relevant sign-on language, so if no description exists in that language, the API can return it as blank. SAP documents this in Note 3394360 and positions broader support as a product gap / feature request topic

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3394360

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Learning Hub Subscription Purchasing Error (VAL_0101)
entry.content:
Hi,

I was in similar situation couple of years back (and last month) when i tried to buy learning hub as an individual. Since you have less than a week to keep current with your certifications, Fastest way to get subscription in by contacting one of your local SAP Learning partners , they can buy subscription with your SAP ID.  Partners usually need 2-3 working days (in India) to get your LHub activated.

If you have already raised a ticket with SAP i guess they can help you by directing you to a learning partner and expedite the purchase and activation.

Someone from SAP please take a note, I think SAP should seriously consider allowing individual users (who are not having a Job like me with no learning subscription from customer/partner) to buy LHub directly. Without this we individuals will end up lose time looking for partners and risk losing our certification validity.

Regards,

Jagan.

entry.title: Re: Automating the Extraction of SAP CI DS security audit...
entry.content:
There is a relevant update on this subject; the CI-DS server version 2603 allows a CSV download of the Security Log content via Administration tab > Security Log tab. This new feature appears on the Kyma platform only, CI-DS Orgs that have not migrated from the original Neo platform will not receive this feature until they migrate to Kyma.

This CSV download still requires an authorized user to access the UI and click a button, but it streamlines access to this security audit log significantly, it does not introduce any API to obtain the audit information.

entry.title: What’s new in Lifecycle Management for SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2602 ?
entry.content:
SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2602

 


This release brings powerful Lifecycle Management (LCM) enhancements that streamline how you manage, access, monitor, and secure your SAP Business One environment,
delivering greater efficiency for admins and a smoother experience for end users. 

Key highlights in FP 2602 include:
  • License consumption tracking in License Control Center
  • Certificate expiration visibility directly in System Landscape Directory
  • DATEV export capability in Web Client, powered by Job Service with the new Job Monitor app
  • Support User Session reminders in Web Client
  • Component version visibility in System Landscape Directory
  • New standalone components: Webhook Messenger and Microsoft 365 Integration Service

and more...

Watch the video below to see these updates in action:
▶️ Latest Lifecycle Management (LCM) enhancements in SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2602

 

Explore the full details in the What’s New guides:

  • What's New (SAP Business One)
  • What's New (SAP Business One, Version for HANA) 

💡Coming soon:  On my next blog I will walk you through the latest Identity and Access Management (IAM) innovations with Feature Pack 2602,  stay tuned!

entry.title: Re: Unable to start the server
entry.content:
Hi @deva346 ,

Hope you resolved the issue or else you can follow below troubleshoot steps .

The cause would be Incomplete Installation ,previous process may not have shut down cleanly or macOS-Specific Permissions

#Verify Server Process Stat
ps aux | grep hybris

#Check if Tomcat ports are in use (default: 9001, 900
lsof -i :9001
lsof -i :9002

#Check for lock file
ls -la hybris/data/

#Clean Server Stat
Force kill any remaining hybris processes

Navigate to platform directory
cd hybris/bin/platform

##Clean temporary files and lock
rm -rf hybris/data/hsqldb/*.lck
rm -rf hybris/data/hsqldb/*.log
rm -rf tomcat/work/*
rm -rf tomcat/temp/*

# Verify script and restore default wrapper
cd hybris/bin/platform/tomcat/bin

# Check if wrapper.sh exists and is executable
ls -la wrapper.sh

# If wrapper.sh is missing or corrupted, restore from commerce suite default
# Compare with a fresh installation or backup

# Ensure execute permissions
chmod +x wrapper.sh
chmod +x ../conf/wrapper.conf
chmod +x ../../hybrisserver.sh

# Verify directory structure
ls -la hybris/bin/platform/tomcat/conf/

Restart Server Properly
Navigate to platform directory
cd hybris/bin/platform

Start server
/hybrisserver.sh start


Regards,

entry.title: S/4HANA vs C4C as Leading System for Business Partner in Financial Services Scenario
entry.content:
Hola expertos,

Me gustaría recibir orientación basada en las mejores prácticas de SAP con respecto a la arquitectura correcta para la creación y replicación de socios comerciales (clientes) entre SAP S/4HANA y SAP Cloud for Customer (C4C).

Contexto:

Actualmente estamos diseñando una solución para una institución financiera (entorno cooperativo/bancario) con las siguientes características:

  • SAP S/4HANA como sistema ERP central (sin MDG implementado)
  • SAP Cloud for Customer (C4C) para procesos de ventas y servicio
  • Integración a través de SAP Integration Suite (CPI)
  • Sólidos requisitos regulatorios y de cumplimiento, que incluyen:
    KYC (Conozca a su cliente),
    AML (Antilavado de dinero),
    PEP (Personas Políticamente Expuestas),
    FATCA/CRS
    , regulaciones APNFD,
    listas negras y listas de vigilancia internacionales,
    relaciones familiares y partes relacionadas,
    beneficiarios con porcentaje de propiedad
    , datos financieros y personales ampliados con múltiples registros.
  • El modelo de socio comercial incluye:
    Relaciones complejas (familia, grupos vinculados, beneficiarios)
    Atributos y validaciones regulatorias
    Extensiones personalizadas para necesidades operativas y de cumplimiento
  • SAP S/4HANA es el sistema donde:
    Procesos financieros
    Datos legales y contractuales
    Lógica empresarial central (banca central)
    Controles de cumplimiento y normativas

son ejecutados y gobernados.

Pregunta:

Desde la perspectiva de la arquitectura y las mejores prácticas de SAP:

¿Cuál es el sistema principal (sistema de registro) recomendado para Business Partner?

  • S/4HANA → C4C (replicación)
  • C4C → S/4HANA
  • Enfoque bidireccional

Preocupaciones específicas:

  1. Gobernanza de datos:
    Sin los ODM, ¿debería S/4HANA seguir siendo el registro maestro?
  2. Cumplimiento normativo:
    En un escenario de servicios financieros, ¿se recomienda centralizar Business Partner en S/4HANA debido a la auditabilidad y el control?
  3. Patrones de integración
    Los escenarios de integración estándar de SAP parecen posicionar a S/4HANA como el sistema backend líder.
    ¿Sigue siendo este el enfoque recomendado para C4C?
  4. Riesgos de usar C4C como sistema de origen
    Inconsistencias de datos
    Conflictos de propiedad
    Limitaciones en el manejo de relaciones complejas y atributos regulatorios
  5. Replicación bidireccional:
    ¿En qué escenarios la recomienda realmente SAP?
    ¿Cuándo debe evitarse?

Nota adicional:

Entendemos que, técnicamente, ambos sistemas pueden crear socios comerciales. Sin embargo, queremos validar el modelo de gobernanza adecuado y la estrategia del sistema principal, teniendo en cuenta especialmente el cumplimiento normativo, la propiedad de los datos y la escalabilidad a largo plazo.

Gracias de antemano por sus aportaciones.

entry.title: Re: CA_OC_OUTPUT_REQUEST_SRV S4 Public Cloud
entry.content:
Good day @Tisha_Haria 

Thank you for your question.

You should not treat CA_OC_OUTPUT_REQUEST_SRV as a released external integration API in Public Cloud. It appears to be an internal OData service that is not released in Public Cloud with a standard predefined communication scenario. In Public Cloud external integrations are supposed to use released APIs and their associated communication scenarios/arrangements.

You cannot grant the missing authorization manually in Public Cloud as you would do in on-premise or private cloud. Your 403 error indicates the service start authorization check is failing for that OData service. In Public Cloud, you do not fix this by directly assigning backend authorization objects to make an internal service consumable from outside.

See the following references for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6d57d4ab71e5408581e2018320a0ce49/8ac548ba2cb04e2bb30d77b59fecb242.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/7139c1648de84c0bbd7637589ed7c92a.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Juntada SAP en Asuncion - Territorio Chopp Las Mercedes
entry.content:
¡Hey SAPeros de Asunción!

¡Ya vamos por la tercera juntada de la comunidad SAP Paraguay! Lo que empezó como una aventura en Territorio Chopp ya se convirtió en una tradición.
Y esta edición tiene sabor especial, porque convocamos a todos: a los veteranos ABAPeros que llevan años escribiendo reports y BAPIs, a los SAPeros de todos los módulos y áreas funcionales... ¡y especialmente a los nuevos RAPeros! que están construyendo con ABAP RAP y CAP, modelando CDS Views, exponiendo OData Services y lanzando apps en BTP como si nada.

El plan es simple: nada de corbatas ni presentaciones formales. Solo buena onda, buena cerveza artesanal y conversaciones reales sobre el ecosistema SAP — desde el clásico SE38 hasta el último release de BTP. Tal vez conozcas a tu próximo colega de proyecto, a un futuro socio, o simplemente pases una noche genial con gente que entiende lo que significa lidiar con un dump a las 11 de la noche. 

La comunidad SAP Paraguay sigue creciendo, juntada a juntada. La comunidad somos todos — clásicos y modernos, funcionales y técnicos, on-premise y cloud.

Detalles importantes:
Fecha: 25 de Marzo de 2026
Hora: 19:30 hs
Lugar: Territorio Chopp Las Mercedes
Ubicación: https://maps.app.goo.gl/Qa8tkumLLcsBJQ2y9

Confirmá tu asistencia en la sección de RSVP.
Nota: Cada uno es responsable de su consumición.

¡No te lo pierdas!
¡Nos vemos pronto! 
Zami

entry.title: Syncronizing the batch Numbers in both EWM & S4

Do the normal batch configuration will work on this case?
S4 - BATCH_CLT

EWM - /SCWM/CHAR
SIMG - SCM EWM - EWM - Cross Process Settings - Batch Management - Batch Management for Decentralized EWM - Define Number Ranges for Batches
Will the NR from different object get updated automatically?

What is the use of SIMG - Logistics General - Batch Management - Batch Number Assignment - Maintain Number Range for External Batch Number Assignment

entry.title: Re: 1H 2026 Product Release- Browse courses or search lea...
entry.content:
Hi @RadhikaD : Below are the responses to your questions.

  1. For now, the search is supported for "Required Learning" , "Important Learning", "Requests for Me", "Invest in Myself" & "Bookmarks" sections where you have the "View All" option. It allows you to filter from the list of results you have when you click on "View All" button.
  2. In this scenario, the search result also depends on the keyword. For example,
    • If the user is searching with English keyword, but since the title is in German, the result wouldn't show up.
    • But, if the user is searching with German keyword even though his preferred language is English, the result would still show up.

entry.title: Re: U.S SECURE 2.0 Act - Section 603
entry.content:
Hello @amrice 

If I understand correctly, you have a scenario where a single plan includes both pre-tax and post-tax contributions, and you're concerned that the catch-up amount should be considered as post-tax but currently is not. Is that the case? The categorization of the catch-up amount as post-tax or pre-tax depends on the Processing Class 65 settings for the wage type in use and whether the plan allows for rollover. Essentially, whether the catch-up amount is treated as post-tax or pre-tax is determined by your system's configuration.

Regarding the 457 catch-up, it is a different type of plan and does not interfere with the 401k catch-up contribution. The 457 catch-up limit is determined by the constant 457CL, whereas the 401k plan's catch-up limit is defined by the constant 401CL. Both constants can be found in view V_T511P.

Best regards,
Gisele

entry.title: BP Extension - Search By Email in ECC 6.0
entry.content:
The Client was having a legacy system where they can search the for particular Vendor if its created or not based on email ID. So moving forward they came up with requirement to centralize the Vendor Management System by migrating all things into ECC.

The first requirement came to make BP screen as it was in legacy system where user can search for Partner numbers created in system based on Email Id provided. I went through many SAP notes and found multiple approaches.

Approach

  • SPRO config – Search Criteria
SAP Customizing Implementation Guide → Logistics - General → Cross-Application Components → SAP Business Partner → Business Partner Search → Define Search Criteria for Business Partner



OR

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide → Logistics - General → Cross-Application Components → SAP Business Partner → Business Partner Search → Basic Setting → Search → Define Search Views

Note: These will only work if the BP Search Framework is fully active

  • BUS config

  • LOCA_CUST

The above 2 options didn’t worked due to lower SAP version. So the only option was to explore LOCA_CUST.

Here is the details of all technical objects created and mapping to LOCA_CUST.

  1. Create a Database View (SE11)
    Since email addresses live in table ADR6, we need a view that joins the BP to its email.
     Tables & Joins:
     BUT000 (Partner) joined to BUT020 (BP/Address Link) on PARTNER = PARTNER.
     BUT020 joined to ADR6 (Email) on ADDRNUMBER = ADDRNUMBER.

     

  2. Create an Elementary Search Help
    Once the view is active, create the search help that the BP transaction will actually display.
    SE11 -> Search Help
     Selection Method: Enter the view you just created (ZBP_EMAIL_SEARCH).
     Search Help Exit: Use F4UT_OPTIMIZE_COLWIDTH (optional, for better UI).
     Parameters:
     SMTP_ADDR: Mark as IMP (Import) and EXP (Export). LPos = 1, SPos = 1.


  3. Append to the Collective Search Help
    Now you must tell the BP transaction to include your new "Email" tab in the locator.
    Open Collective Search Help BUPA_ALL
    Go to the Included Search Helps tab.
    Click the Append Search Help button (F5).
    Create a new Append . In the "Included Search Helps" of your append, add your elementary search help

    Click Parameter Assignment to map the PARTNER field from your search help to the collective search help.



  4.  Verification
    After activating everything:
    Run transaction BP.
    In the Locator (left-hand side), click the "Find By" dropdown.
    You should now see your new entry ( with the Z name).

    NOTE: However if you click on the new field it will throw error “Program name and Screen are not configured”     

  5.  Pass the value of email field in Standard SAP flow of BP transaction
    Create Append structure in BUS_JOEL_SEARCH. SE11 ->  BUS_JOEL_SEARCH - >Append Structure(F5)


  6. Here comes the Important Step for mapping to SAP hook using ABAP code. This FM will capture the data of new field and integrate it into the standard BP process for this we need to use the OOPS concept and include some standard processing code from LBUPA_DIALOG_SEARCHF02 and LBUS_LOCATORO01.
  7. Create a Function Group. SE37-> Goto-> Function Group -> Create Group .


  8. Click on Master Program of function group and define a local class in TOP include along with the Global variable for fetching the value of email in GV_EMAIL.
FUNCTION-POOL ZBP_FMGRP_BUPA.               "MESSAGE-ID ..

* INCLUDE LZBP_FMGRP_BUPAD...                " Local class definition
TABLES bus_joel_search.

DATA: gs_dynpro_id TYPE bus_screen-area,
      gv_email type adr6-smtp_addr. " capture screen value

CLASS lcl_searcher DEFINITION FINAL.

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    CLASS-METHODS:
    select_by_email
    IMPORTING iv_search  TYPE REF TO cl_bus_locator_search
              iv_email TYPE bus_joel_search-smtp_addr
              EXCEPTIONS search_values_missing.

ENDCLASS.
  • Create a Copy FM from Standard BUPA_DIALOG_SEARCH into a  Z Function module. Follow the below code to fetch the partner from the view created in STEP1 based on input from email of BP screen.
FUNCTION zbp_bupa_dialog_emailrch.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     REFERENCE(IV_SEARCH) TYPE REF TO  CL_BUS_LOCATOR_SEARCH
*"  EXCEPTIONS
*"      SEARCH_VALUES_MISSING
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------

* Local data.
  DATA: ls_search_fields TYPE bus_joel_search. 
* Convert generic container into specific fields.
  ls_search_fields = iv_search->gv_search_fields.           

* Choose the select.
  CASE iv_search->gv_search_id.

*   The empty search?
    WHEN space.
      CALL METHOD lcl_searcher=>select_by_nothing
                  EXPORTING iv_search = iv_search.

    WHEN 'ZBP_EMAIL_SRCHELP'.    "name of elementary search help
      CALL METHOD lcl_searcher=>select_by_email
        EXPORTING
          iv_search             = iv_search
          iv_email              = ls_search_fields-smtp_addr
        EXCEPTIONS
          search_values_missing = 1.

    WHEN OTHERS.
      CLEAR sy-subrc.
  ENDCASE.
*
  CASE sy-subrc.
    WHEN 1.
      RAISE search_values_missing.
  ENDCASE.

ENDFUNCTION.

​
  • Implement the method by creating a new include in Master Program.

    with the below code the Partner GUID is passed back again to BP framework to display the result of partner number based on the user input of email id. 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&  Include           ZBP_FMGRP_BUPA01
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
CLASS lcl_searcher IMPLEMENTATION.
*---------------------------------------------------------------------
  METHOD select_by_email.
*   Local data.

    DATA: lv_partner_type       TYPE bus_partner-type,
          lr_email TYPE RANGE OF adr6-smtp_addr,
          lt_partner_guids  TYPE bus_partner-guid_table.


    IF gv_email NE space.
      SELECT partner_guid INTO TABLE lt_partner_guids
               FROM zbp_email_search
               UP TO iv_search->gv_maximum_rows ROWS
               WHERE smtp_addr EQ gv_email.
*      BREAK-POINT.

      IF sy-subrc NE 0.
        TRANSLATE gv_email TO LOWER CASE.
        SELECT partner_guid INTO TABLE lt_partner_guids
             FROM zbp_email_search
             UP TO iv_search->gv_maximum_rows ROWS
             WHERE smtp_addr EQ gv_email.
      ENDIF.

    ENDIF.
*   Pass result table
    CALL METHOD cl_bupa_dialog_searcher=>add_partner_guids_to_result
      EXPORTING
        it_partner_guids         = lt_partner_guids
        iv_filter_by_search_type = space
        iv_search                = iv_search.
  ENDMETHOD.                    "select_by_email

ENDCLASS.                    "lcl_searcher IMPLEMENTATION
  • Create a new screen by Right click on Function group -> Create->Screen

    Note: GV_EMAIL is the same which is declared in step 8.Don’t forget to make the text box as scrollable otherwise you may loose data .

  • Double click on PBO and PAI and create new include in Master Program of function group. And do the following code. This code helps to pass the necessary information of standard processing copied from LBUPA_DIALOG_SEARCHF02 and LBUS_LOCATORO01.
----------------------------------------------------------------------*
***INCLUDE LZBP_FMGRP_BUPAO01.
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Module  DYNPRO_PBO  OUTPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
MODULE dynpro_pbo OUTPUT.
  gs_dynpro_id-program_name = sy-repid.
  gs_dynpro_id-dynpro_number = sy-dynnr.

  CALL METHOD cl_bus_abstract_screen=>dynpro_pbo
              EXPORTING iv_program_name = gs_dynpro_id-program_name
                        iv_dynpro_number = gs_dynpro_id-dynpro_number.

ENDMODULE.                 " DYNPRO_PBO  OUTPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Module  DYNPRO_PAI  INPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
MODULE dynpro_pai INPUT.
  gs_dynpro_id-program_name = sy-repid.
  gs_dynpro_id-dynpro_number = sy-dynnr.

  CALL METHOD cl_bus_abstract_screen=>dynpro_pai
              EXPORTING iv_program_name = gs_dynpro_id-program_name
                        iv_dynpro_number = gs_dynpro_id-dynpro_number.
ENDMODULE.                 " DYNPRO_PAI  INPUT
​
  • Create a new include in Master Program of function group and enter the below code. These subroutines are called from the BUS_JEOL_LOCATOR framework hence it is required in our FM.
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       FORM bus_locator_get_search_fields                            *
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*

FORM bus_locator_get_search_fields                          "#EC CALLED
     USING    iv_search_application TYPE bus_locator-search_application
     CHANGING ev_search_fields      TYPE bus_locator-search_fields.

 CASE iv_search_application.
    WHEN cl_bupa_dialog_searcher=>gc_search_application_bupa.
      ev_search_fields = bus_joel_search."#EC ENHOK
  ENDCASE.

ENDFORM.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       FORM bus_locator_set_search_fields                            *
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*

FORM bus_locator_set_search_fields                          "#EC CALLED
     USING    iv_search_application TYPE bus_locator-search_application
              iv_search_fields      TYPE bus_locator-search_fields.

  CASE iv_search_application.
    WHEN cl_bupa_dialog_searcher=>gc_search_application_bupa.
      bus_joel_search = iv_search_fields. "#EC ENHOK
  ENDCASE.

ENDFORM.
  • Functional Configuration in LOCA_CUST

    Create new entry under Search by ID

    Search for the Search Help created in Step gy doing an F4 on Locator Search ID.

    Screen text would be displayed on the drop of Find by screen of BP .Now do F4 for FM and Program name and enter screen number created in step 11 the details. That’s the end for the required config for mapping the new field to BP screen.



  • Let’s test
    Enter Tcode BP. Click on BY dropdown to see the new field available under name Email.

    Enter any relevant email Id to find the respective BP . You can find the BP from the view created in Step 1 as well.

    Result

entry.title: Architecting a Clean-Core, Agentic Exception-Handling Pattern for SAP Order Fulfillment
entry.content:
Business scenario at a glance

Real-world problem: A customer order is accepted in SAP S/4HANA, but a downstream shortage, carrier constraint, or credit/logistics exception threatens the promised delivery date. The enterprise wants faster, AI-assisted resolution without pushing new custom logic into the ERP core. This is where Clean Core 2.0 and Agentic AI become one architecture pattern rather than two separate buzzwords [1]–[7].

Architectural point: S/4HANA remains the system of record for sales orders, ATP, pricing, delivery, and financial posting. The reasoning layer sits side-by-side on SAP BTP, consumes released APIs and business events, grounds recommendations on trusted enterprise context, and writes back only through governed interfaces [1], [3]–[8].

 

Figure 1. Clean-core responsibility split for order-fulfillment exceptions

The figure above makes the design boundary explicit: the ERP core executes the business transaction, while the side-by-side layer performs contextual reasoning and orchestrates bounded actions. That partition aligns with SAP clean-core extensibility guidance and reduces the chance that AI becomes another form of hidden customization [1], [2].

Runtime architecture for the real scenario

At runtime, the architecture works in six steps. First, S/4HANA creates or updates the sales order and produces the standard business outcome. Second, an approved event or API call exposes the change to SAP Integration Suite and, where needed, advanced event mesh. Third, a Joule-based or SAP BTP agent service classifies the situation as a fulfillment exception. Fourth, the agent gathers business context from governed sources such as policy documents, logistics rules, customer commitments, and curated data products. Fifth, it proposes a bounded action, such as split shipment, alternate source proposal, or carrier-switch recommendation. Sixth, the approved action is written back through released APIs or workflow paths into the transactional landscape [3]–[9].

Figure 2. SAP agentic exception-handling runtime architecture

This layered model matters because it defines where trust controls sit. SAP’s Joule architecture, grounding patterns in SAP AI Core, and responsible AI guidance all point toward a model in which enterprise context, policy, and approval must be explicit architectural controls rather than afterthoughts [3]–[7].

Concrete order-to-cash example

Example: A distributor promises a same-week delivery for a high-priority customer order. Minutes later, inventory in the planned warehouse is consumed by another allocation, and the preferred carrier misses its cut-off window. In older landscapes, planners would manually piece together ATP, stock-in-transit, customer priority, freight rules, and contractual penalties across multiple screens. In the target design, the agent assembles that same context automatically and recommends one of three pre-approved actions: (1) partial shipment now plus backorder, (2) reallocation from a secondary warehouse, or (3) carrier substitution with cost impact shown to the approver [1], [3]–[9].

Figure 3. Real-world order-fulfillment exception flow

Final Thoughts

The business case shared above answers a concrete architecture broader question: what should stay in the ERP core, what should move to a side-by-side intelligence layer, and how do we preserve compliance, upgradeability, and operational control? The scenario is representative of real order-to-cash programs in manufacturing, distribution, and consumer products, where exception handling speed directly affects OTIF, logistics cost, and customer experience.

References

[1] SAP, 'Clean core extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud,' 2024. URL: https://www.sap.com/docs/download/2024/09/20aece06-d87e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.pdf

[2] SAP, 'RISE with SAP | ERP Clean Core Strategy,' 2025. URL: https://www.sap.com/products/erp/rise/methodology/clean-core.html

[3] SAP Learning, 'Understanding Joule Architecture and Extensibility,' 2025. URL: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/provisioning-and-implementing-joule/understanding-joule-architecture-and-extensibility

[4] SAP Developers, 'Orchestration with Grounding Capabilities in SAP AI Core,' 2025. URL: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-orchestration-grounding..html

[5] SAP, 'Responsible AI,' 2025. URL: https://www.sap.com/products/artificial-intelligence/ai-ethics.html

[6] SAP, 'SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh,' 2025. URL: https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/integration-suite/advanced-event-mesh.html

[7] SAP Help Portal, 'SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh Releases,' 2026. URL: https://help.pubsub.em.services.cloud.sap/Release-Notes/PubSub-Cloud-Release-Notes.htm

[8] SAP TechEd workshop, 'Real-time data for AI agents: Grounding with event-driven architecture,' 2025. URL: https://github.com/SAP-samples/teched2025-IN162

[9] SAP News Center, 'Shape the Future of Intelligent Applications with SAP Business Data Cloud,' May 21, 2025. URL: https://news.sap.com/2025/05/future-intelligent-applications-sap-business-data-cloud/
